hotham.narrative: This is worth coming back for!  We've had an average of 17 cm of cold snow overnight and there is still the odd burst being blown through by the strong northwest wind this morning.  The current temperature is minus 4, and the forecast is calling for the snow showers to intensify later in the day. This snow has certainly improved the cover below the road and I'm hopeful that we'll be able to get The Village Chair running today.  An assessment will be made at first light, and if it's a goer, the operations teams will be doing what they can to get it spinning as soon as possible.  And I suspect the strong wind may play havoc with the upper chair lifts, so keep a close eye on the lift status this morning.    I'll hopefully see you cutting a few laps of The Village Loop in good snow conditions today, but regardless of how things play out in the short term, we are definitely heading in the right direction.  Enjoy. Cheers Bill

This snow has certainly improved the cover below the road and I'm hopeful that we'll be able to get The Village Chair running today.  An assessment will be made at first light, and if it's a goer, the operations teams will be doing what they can to get it spinning as soon as possible.  And I suspect the strong wind may play havoc with the upper chair lifts, so keep a close eye on the lift status this morning.    I'll hopefully see you cutting a few laps of The Village Loop in good snow conditions today, but regardless of how things play out in the short term, we are definitely heading in the right direction.  Enjoy. Cheers Bill

perisher.narrative: 15cm overnight and more on the way!. The snowstorm arrived at Perisher yesterday afternoon and has not stopped! Since yesterday,15cm of snow has fallen and more is expected today. Check out the snowstake to see it for yourself! In addition to the natural snowfall, the snow guns were also on last night to lend a helping hand, running from 3:00am and still going at time of writing. Today at Perisher is forecast to be cloudy with more snow showers. Winds are picking up from yesterday, with north westerly winds travelling between 33-40km/h with stronger gusts expected. Stronger winds may impact lift operations today, so be sure to download the Perisher App or keep an eye on the lift report for real-time updates. Yesterday we saw more lifts and terrain open, including the Summit Quad Chair, bringing our current lift offering to 19 lifts. We'll continue to open more terrain as soon as it's possible and when it's safe to do so... stay tuned! Welcome to all of our guests competing in The Scots Race today - we wish you the best of luck in your races! To our other guests, please note that The Scots Race will take place on Front Valley, so it might be a good day to check out areas like Smiggin Holes. Off the slopes today, Jax Bar will see Jack Billman and Vibe Duo take the stage at 11:00am and 3:00pm respectively. Be sure to catch some of the performances in between runs! The forecast is calling for more snow, so be sure to check the snow cams throughout the day and come back for tomorrow's morning snow report!

latest.narrative: 15cm overnight and more on the way!. The snowstorm arrived at Perisher yesterday afternoon and has not stopped! Since yesterday,15cm of snow has fallen and more is expected today. Check out the snowstake to see it for yourself! In addition to the natural snowfall, the snow guns were also on last night to lend a helping hand, running from 3:00am and still going at time of writing. Today at Perisher is forecast to be cloudy with more snow showers. Winds are picking up from yesterday, with north westerly winds travelling between 33-40km/h with stronger gusts expected. Stronger winds may impact lift operations today, so be sure to download the Perisher App or keep an eye on the lift report for real-time updates. Yesterday we saw more lifts and terrain open, including the Summit Quad Chair, bringing our current lift offering to 19 lifts. We'll continue to open more terrain as soon as it's possible and when it's safe to do so... stay tuned! Welcome to all of our guests competing in The Scots Race today - we wish you the best of luck in your races! To our other guests, please note that The Scots Race will take place on Front Valley, so it might be a good day to check out areas like Smiggin Holes. Off the slopes today, Jax Bar will see Jack Billman and Vibe Duo take the stage at 11:00am and 3:00pm respectively. Be sure to catch some of the performances in between runs! The forecast is calling for more snow, so be sure to check the snow cams throughout the day and come back for tomorrow's morning snow report!

latest.narrative: This is worth coming back for!  We've had an average of 17 cm of cold snow overnight and there is still the odd burst being blown through by the strong northwest wind this morning.  The current temperature is minus 4, and the forecast is calling for the snow showers to intensify later in the day. This snow has certainly improved the cover below the road and I'm hopeful that we'll be able to get The Village Chair running today.  An assessment will be made at first light, and if it's a goer, the operations teams will be doing what they can to get it spinning as soon as possible.  And I suspect the strong wind may play havoc with the upper chair lifts, so keep a close eye on the lift status this morning.    I'll hopefully see you cutting a few laps of The Village Loop in good snow conditions today, but regardless of how things play out in the short term, we are definitely heading in the right direction.  Enjoy. Cheers Bill

It is a winter wonderland at Falls today and it is still coming down! Please take it easy on the roads getting up and ensure you are carrying chains. Currently chains will be fitted to 2WDs, please drive slowly and to the conditions when visiting.
